instruction: What can you tell me about CVE-2024-57880?
input:
What can you tell me about CVE-2024-57880?


The user mentioned a CVE, the following CVE information is available:

{"CVE_ID": "CVE-2024-57880", "Description": "In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ASoC: Intel: sof_sdw: Add space for a terminator into DAIs array

The code uses the initialised member of the asoc_sdw_dailink struct to
determine if a member of the array is in use. However in the case the
array is completely full this will lead to an access 1 past the end of
the array, expand the array by one entry to include a space for a
terminator.", "CWE Name": "NVD-CWE-noinfo", "CWE Description": "N/A", "Associated MITRE ATT&CK Technique": "T1068", "Severity": "MEDIUM", "Known Vulnerable Software": "["{'operator': 'OR', 'negate': False, 'cpeMatch': [{'vulnerable': True, 'criteria': 'cpe:2.3:o:linux:linux_kernel:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*', 'versionStartIncluding': '6.10', 'versionEndExcluding': '6.12.6', 'matchCriteriaId': 'EFB33A9C-69D1-4691-B0BD-2D5C9590E239'}, {'vulnerable': True, 'criteria': 'cpe:2.3:o:linux:linux_kernel:6.13:rc1:*:*:*:*:*:*', 'matchCriteriaId': '62567B3C-6CEE-46D0-BC2E-B3717FBF7D13'}, {'vulnerable': True, 'criteria': 'cpe:2.3:o:linux:linux_kernel:6.13:rc2:*:*:*:*:*:*', 'matchCriteriaId': '5A073481-106D-4B15-B4C7-FB0213B8E1D4'}]}"]", "Mitigation Techniques": "M1051, M1050, M1048, M1019, M1038"}
output:
CVE-2024-57880 addresses a vulnerability in the Linux kernel specifically related to the ASoC Intel sof_sdw component. The issue occurs due to an array bounds error where the code attempts to access an element past the end of a fully populated array, leading to potential memory access violations. This is mitigated by adding a space for a terminator in the array.

The vulnerability is categorized under "NVD-CWE-noinfo", indicating a lack of detailed categorization information. It is considered to have a MEDIUM severity level. Potential impacts include unauthorized access leading to system compromise.

Affected software includes Linux kernels from version 6.10 up to 6.12.6 and the early release candidates of version 6.13. Effective mitigation techniques involve implementing controls such as M1051, M1050, M1048, M1019, and M1038. The vulnerability is associated with MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1068, which relates to exploitation for privilege escalation.
